questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f:\left[\frac{-1}{2}, 0\right] \rightarrow B$ defined by $f(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(4 x^2+3 x\right)$ is onto, then the set $B$ is $\left[\frac{\pi}{2}, \pi-\cos ^{-1} \frac{a}{b}\right]$, where $a$ and $b$ are co-prime, then find the value of $(a+b)$.
questions.solution:
Since $f(x)$ is onto hence range of $f(x)$ equals co-domain 
Now range of $4 x^2+3 x$ in $\left[\frac{-1}{2}, 0\right]$ is $\left[\frac{-9}{16}, 0\right]$ 
Hence, range of $f(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(4 x^2+3 x\right)$ is $\left[\frac{\pi}{2}, \pi-\cos ^{-1} \frac{9}{16}\right]$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: Let $M$ be the greatest and $m$ be the least value of $\sqrt{\sin ^{-1} x}+\sqrt{\cos ^{-1} x}$, then find the value of $(M / m)^4$.
questions.solution:
$$
y=\sqrt{t}+\sqrt{(\pi / 2)-t} \text { where } t=\sin ^{-1} x \in[0, \pi / 2]
$$

Now $y_{\min }=1$ at $x=0$ and $y_{\max }=\sqrt{2}$ at $t=\frac{1}{2}$

questions.q: The number of real solutions of $\cos ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} 2 x=-\pi$ is
questions.solution:

$\cos ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} 2 x=-\pi \Rightarrow$ no solution

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For $y=\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{\log e x^2}{\log e / x^2}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3+2 \ln x}{1-6 \ln x}\right)$, the value of $\frac{d^2 y}{d x^2}$ at $x=1$, is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution:
The given function is
$y=\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{1+2 \ln x}{1-2 \ln x}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3+2 \ln x}{1-6 \ln x}\right)$

Let $\tan \theta=2 \ln x, \tan \phi=3$ so that
$\begin{aligned}
y & =\cot ^{-1}\left(\frac{\tan \frac{\pi}{4}+\tan \theta}{1-\tan \frac{\pi}{4} \tan \theta}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\tan \phi+\tan \theta}{1-\tan \phi \tan \theta}\right) \\
& =\cot ^{-1}\left(\tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta\right)\right)+\tan ^{-1}(\tan (\phi+\theta)) \\
& =\frac{\pi}{2}-\left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta\right)+\phi+\theta=\frac{\pi}{4}+\phi=\frac{\pi}{4}+\tan ^{-1} 3=\text { constant } \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \frac{\mathrm{d}^2 \mathrm{y}}{\mathrm{dx}^2}=0
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The value of the expression $\tan \left(\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{9}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{8}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{25}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{18}\right)+\ldots \ldots \infty\right)$, is:
questions.solution:
Let $S=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{9}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{8}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{25}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{18}\right)+\ldots \ldots \infty$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& =\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{4}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{9}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{16}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{25}\right)+\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{36}\right)+\ldots \ldots \infty \\
T_n & =\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{(n+1)^2}\right) \\
S & =\sum_{r=1}^{\infty} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2}{r^2+2 r+1}\right)=\sum_{r=1}^{\infty} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{(r+2)-r}{1+r(r+2)}\right)=\sum_{r=1}^{\infty} \tan ^{-1}(r+2)-\tan ^{-1}(r) \\
S & =\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{3 n^2+7 n}{n^2+9 n+10}\right)=\tan ^{-1}(3)
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence, $\tan S=\tan \left(\tan ^{-1} 3\right)=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Find the sum of squares of the solution of the equation
$$
\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}-2 \sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x^2}\right)
$$
questions.solution:
(i) If $x \geq 1,2 \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}-2\left(\pi-2 \tan ^{-1} x\right) \Rightarrow 2 \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{-3 \pi}{2} \Rightarrow$ no solution.
(ii) If $0 \leq x < 1,2 \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}-2\left(2 \tan ^{-1} x\right) \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{12} \Rightarrow x=2-\sqrt{3}$
(iii) If $-1 \leq x < 0,-2 \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}-2\left(2 \tan ^{-1} x\right) \Rightarrow 2 \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2} \Rightarrow x=1$ (not possible)
(iv) If $x < -1,-2 \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{\pi}{2}-2\left(-\pi-2 \tan ^{-1} x\right) \Rightarrow 6 \tan ^{-1} x=\frac{-5 \pi}{2} \Rightarrow x=-2-\sqrt{3}$
$\therefore$ Sum of square $=14$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f: R \rightarrow R, f(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right)+\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x^2}\right)$
If the value of $f(\sqrt{3})+f(-\ln 2)+f(1)+f(\ln 3)$ is equal to $k \pi(k \in W)$, then find the value of $k$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & f(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{1-x^2}{1+x^2}\right)+\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x}{1+x^2}\right) \\ & f(x)=\left[\begin{array}{cc}-\pi-4 \tan ^{-1} x, & x < -1 \\ 0, & -1 \leq x < 0 \\ 4 \tan ^{-1} x, & 0 \leq x < 1 \\ \pi, & 1 \leq x\end{array}\right.\end{aligned}$
$f(\sqrt{3})+f(-\ln 2)+f(1)+f(\ln 3)=\pi+0+\pi+\pi=3 \pi$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
In $\triangle A B C$, let $a$ and $b$ be minimum and maximum values of the function $f(x)=\sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} x$ respectively and $\cos A=\frac{3}{\sqrt{10}}$. If $r=\frac{p \pi}{q(q+\sqrt{10})}$ where $p, q$ are co-prime numbers, then find the value of $(p+q)$.
[Note: Symbols used have usual meaning in $\triangle A B C$ ]
questions.solution:
$$
f(x)=\sin ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} x
$$
Range of $f(x)=\left[\frac{\pi}{4}, \frac{3 \pi}{4}\right] \Rightarrow a=\frac{\pi}{4}$ and $b=\frac{3 \pi}{4}$
Applying, $\cos A=\frac{b^2+c^2-a^2}{2 b c}$
$$
\frac{3}{\sqrt{10}}=\frac{\frac{9 \pi^2}{16}+c^2-\frac{\pi^2}{16}}{2 \cdot\left(\frac{3 \pi}{4}\right) \cdot c} \Rightarrow c=\frac{\sqrt{10} \pi}{4}, \frac{\pi}{\sqrt{10}} \text { (rejected) }
$$
Here, $a^2+b^2=c^2 \Rightarrow \angle C=90^{\circ}$
$$
r=(s-c) \tan \frac{C}{2}=\left(\frac{\pi+\frac{\sqrt{10} \pi}{4}}{2}-\frac{\sqrt{10} \pi}{4}\right) \times 1=\frac{\pi(4-\sqrt{10})}{8}=\frac{6 \pi}{8(4-\sqrt{10})} \equiv \frac{p \pi}{q(q+\sqrt{10})}
$$
$\therefore p=3$ and $q=4 \Rightarrow p+q=7$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q:
Consider, $f(x)=\frac{|x-4|}{|x|+1}$. If sum of all distinct possible values of $\sin ^{-1}(\sin [f(x)])$ is $a \pi+b$ then find the absolute value of $(a+b)$.
[Note: $[z]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $z$.]
